title: A supersymmetric study of charge and spin transport in Weyl semimetals under axionic electrodynamic response
authors: J. C. Pérez-Pedraza, A. Martín-Ruiz, L. F. Urrutia
abstract: We investigate the charge and spin transport properties of a Weyl semimetal under an external magnetic field using a low-energy effective theory. By performing a chiral transformation, we remove the axial term from the fermionic Lagrangian, while its physical effects are retained through the system's electromagnetic response. This response, derived from integrating out the fermionic degrees of freedom, takes the form of axionic electrodynamics and enters the Dirac equation via minimal coupling. The resulting dynamics separate naturally into magnetic and electric components: the magnetic sector admits a supersymmetric factorization, while the electric part exhibits a PT-supersymmetric structure. Robin boundary conditions are applied to the spinor, setting the energy spectrum and defining exact spinor solutions. We obtain the chiral projections of probability and current densities, exhibiting a chiral imbalance in the material. We show that in the x-direction, only one chirality contributes to the chiral current ($j^x_l = 0$). Spin density and currents where also computed. Our results demonstrate how the interplay between axionic response and supersymmetry governs transport phenomena in our specific setup, offering novel insights into the effective field theory description of Weyl semimetals.
summary: This paper explores how the interplay between axionic electrodynamics and supersymmetry governs transport in Weyl semimetals. By using supersymmetric quantum mechanics, the authors solve the Dirac equation to reveal how magnetic and electric fields induce chiral imbalances and specific spin currents. The results provide a theoretical framework for understanding the effective field theory of Weyl semimetals under external magnetic influence.
main_problem: Investigating the charge and spin transport properties of Weyl semimetals under an external magnetic field, specifically focusing on the effects of axionic electrodynamic response.
main_result: The study demonstrates that the system's dynamics separate into a magnetic sector with supersymmetric factorization and an electric sector with PT-supersymmetric structure, leading to a chiral imbalance and specific spin-current patterns.
method: The authors use a low-energy effective Dirac-Weyl theory, applying chiral transformations, supersymmetric quantum mechanics (SUSY QM), and solving the resulting equations using tri-confluent Heun functions and numerical methods.
model_or_system: A Weyl semimetal (WSM) modeled as a plane slab of thickness 2L in a uniform external magnetic field, characterized by axion electrodynamics where the axial term is replaced by an effective electromagnetic response.
key_observables: Chiral projections of probability and current densities, spin density, spin currents, and axial probability density.
important_parameters: Fermi velocity (vF), axial 4-vector (b), magnetic field (B0), slab thickness (L), and the axion-like field theta(x).
main_assumptions: Low-energy approximation near Weyl nodes, neglect of lattice regularization and band curvature, and a one-particle domain where the axial anomaly is absent.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the WSM slab; Figures 5-6 show energy eigenvalues as a function of quantum numbers and magnetic field; Figures 7-10 display probability and current densities for different chiralities.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the WSM Hamiltonian and applying a chiral transformation to derive axion electrodynamics, then proceeds to decouple the Dirac equation into magnetic and electric sectors, solves these sectors using SUSY and PT-SUSY techniques (including Heun functions), and concludes with the calculation of transport observables and conservation laws.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. System under study consisting of a semi-infinite slab of a Weyl semimetal of thickness 2L in z-direction immersed in an external magnetic field in z-direction. The axion magnetoelectric response in the material induces a non-homogeneous electric field in the same direction. We consider the interior problem only, i.e. in the region −L < z < +L.
FIG. 2. Potentials of the magnetic and electric Hamiltonians for the case Ax(y) = −B0y and ϕ(z) = −ρ0

FIG. 3. The dimensionless energy eigenvalues εn,m, for n = 0, for the conditions Z2 = 0 (solid, black lines) and Z1 = 0 (dashed, red lines). The parameters are B0 = 1.0 T, b = 0.26 nm−1 and L = 100 nm.

FIG. 4. The dimensionless energy eigenvalues εn,m, for n = 0, for the conditions a) Z2 = 0 and b) Z1 = 0, for magnetic fields B0 = 1T (solid, black lines) and B0 = 4T (dashed, blue lines), respectively. The remaining parameters are b = 0.26 nm−1 and L = 100 nm.

FIG. 5. The dimensionless energy eigenvalues εn,m, for m = −2, −1, 0, 1, 2 (in ascendant order) as a function of the quantum number n. a) Complete spectrum (even and odd n). Black solid lines represent the roots of the condition Z2 = 0, while red dashed lines represent the roots of the condition Z2 = 0. b) Even n (black solid) and odd n (red dashed) coming from the condition Z2 = 0. In this plot the m = 0 roots are not included to avoid image saturation.
